clusters.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet

versions.title: Sri Lanka’s missing Balance Sheet
versions.deck: Sri Lanka’s crisis is often described in familiar terms: excessive borrowing, weak fiscal discipline and unsustainable debt. This diagnosis is not wrong. But it is incomplete. At its core, the crisis reflects a deeper issue in how sovereign financial positions are assessed and managed. This leads to the conclusion that loadings are likely to be down 8.8 million bpd from prewar levels.
